Review urgently if Breathlessness, Agitation, Pain, Vomiting or Seizures
Educated about  EOL and treatment options
Maintain skin care and oral hygiene
Advised daily sponge bath / bathroom bath followed by moisturizer
Reposition patient every 2 hours to prevent pressure sores
Continue regular bowel care and monitor constipation
Observe for Catheter blockage, leakage, foul smell, bleeding or sediments
Advised use of air mattress for pressure sore prevention
Monitor for skin redness, pressure areas, or ulcer progression
Regular bowel care and review if constipation persists more than 3 days
Explained to the bystander that there is a risk of Aspiration pneumonia, Intracranial bleed, Cardiac arrest & Sudden death
Light activity only — avoid strenuous exercise
Avoid spicy, oily, and junk food
Warm salt-water gargling 3 times a day
Reduce stress — practice deep breathing or meditation
Monitor blood pressure daily at home
Elevate the affected limb while resting
Use ice pack for 15 min, 3 times a day
Emergency: report to ER if symptoms worsen
